name: Black Goma Asparagus (Choy)
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the asparagus and discard the tough ends by breaking off and removing them.
2. Cut the stalks into 1-1/2-inch sections.
3. If using young, tender asparagus, blanch it briefly in boiling water and rinse under cold water.
4. For mature asparagus, parboil for 1 minute or until bright green, then rinse immediately under cold water and drain.
5. Do not blanch or parboil the tips.
6. In a bowl, combine chicken broth, soy sauce, and sugar.
7. Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat until almost smoking.
8. Add salt and minced ginger; stir-fry for 1 minute.
9. Add asparagus and stir-fry until heated through. Adjust the heat if necessary to avoid scorching.
10. Pour in the broth mixture and heat quickly, covered, on medium heat for 2 minutes.
11. Sprinkle with black goma (or black sesame seeds) before serving.
